hero.title: Operational control for active projects.
hero.subtitle: When projects run across Excel, email, and fragmented knowledge, execution friction grows. Our software creates clear ownership, reliable capacity planning, and visibility into status, risk, and margin.

valueProps.heading: Where operational control breaks today
valueProps.subtitle: Familiar patterns in day-to-day project delivery

items.title: Excel chaos instead of shared visibility
items.body: Project status lives in multiple files with inconsistent updates.

items.title: Scattered workflows
items.body: Information is split across email, DMS, PM tools, and people's heads.

items.title: Unclear responsibilities
items.body: Ownership and due dates are not consistently documented or traceable.

items.title: Weak project handoffs
items.body: Context and decisions are lost between teams and roles.

items.title: Risks appear too late
items.body: Schedule, effort, and margin deviations surface when corrective action is already expensive.

features.heading: An operating system for project execution
features.subtitle: Built for project control, capacity, and profitability

items.title: Project control with clear ownership
items.body: Tasks, decisions, and open issues are assigned by phase and role.

items.title: Structured handoffs
items.body: Handoffs between management, project leads, and delivery teams are standardized and traceable.

items.title: Continuous capacity and risk visibility
items.body: Resource bottlenecks, schedule risks, and delivery conflicts become visible early.

items.title: Profitability in daily operations
items.body: Effort and margin deviations are visible continuously, not only after project close.

deepDive.heading: How a pilot works
deepDive.subtitle: Practical, focused, based on real project data

steps.title: Baseline assessment
steps.body: Together, we identify where control is currently lost across tools and roles.

steps.title: Core process mapping
steps.body: Project status, ownership, handoffs, and capacity are modeled in one structured flow.

steps.title: Live test in operations
steps.body: You validate the approach on real projects and evaluate impact, effort, and fit.

useCases.heading: Who this is for
useCases.subtitle: For firms running multiple projects in parallel under coordination pressure

items.title: Managing directors
items.body: Need reliable visibility across project status, utilization, risks, and contribution margin.

items.title: Department heads
items.body: Coordinate resources across projects and need to set priorities on solid operational data.

items.title: Project managers
items.body: Need clear ownership and clean handoffs to keep schedule and budget execution stable.

integrations.heading: Connected to existing tools
integrations.subtitle: Not a replacement for everything, but a control layer

security.heading: Concrete operational outcomes
security.subtitle: Improvements that are visible in day-to-day delivery

items.title: Earlier escalation instead of firefighting
items.body: Risks surface before schedule slippage and margin loss occur.

items.title: Less friction in coordination and handoffs
items.body: Fewer follow-ups, less duplicate work, less information loss.

items.title: More reliable management decisions
items.body: Leaders decide based on one shared and current operational view.

metrics.heading: What you gain operationally
metrics.subtitle: 4 core effects on delivery control and profitability

metrics.label: Ownership clarity
metrics.description: Responsibilities and next steps are explicit by project phase.

metrics.label: Risk visibility
metrics.description: Schedule, capacity, and financial risks become visible in time.

metrics.label: Control loss
metrics.description: Handoffs and coordination run in a structured process instead of ad hoc.

metrics.label: Project margin
metrics.description: Deviation is addressed earlier, before it erodes project results.

pricing.heading: Pilot before full rollout
pricing.subtitle: For demand testing, fit and impact matter most

plans.name: Pilot phase
features: Mapping of core project and capacity workflows
Joint evaluation of impact and fit

items.question: Does this replace our existing ERP?
items.answer: No. The focus is operational project control, not a generic ERP replacement.

items.question: Do we need to replace all current tools?
items.answer: No. The software is designed as a control layer on top of existing tools.

items.question: Who is this relevant for?
items.answer: Planning and engineering offices running multiple active projects with high coordination load.

testimonials.heading: Voices from pilot discussions
testimonials.subtitle: Typical feedback from the target audience

testimonials.quote: This is exactly the gap we have between project management and department control.
testimonials.name: Managing Director

testimonials.quote: If we see risks two weeks earlier, we protect real margin.
testimonials.name: Department Head

testimonials.quote: For us, clear ownership and structured handoffs are the key.
testimonials.name: Project Manager

finalCta.heading: Test operational control in your own office
finalCta.subtitle: We are onboarding pilot partners for early feedback. In a short conversation, we assess whether the approach fits your project landscape.
